evals.prompt: I want to run DSPy with a local model. I have Ollama installed with llama3.1. How do I connect DSPy to it?
evals.expected_output: Configures dspy.LM with ollama_chat provider and sets num_ctx

assertions.name: sets_empty_api_key
assertions.description: Sets api_key='' (empty string, required by LiteLLM)

assertions.name: sets_num_ctx
assertions.description: Sets num_ctx explicitly rather than relying on Ollama 4096 default

assertions.name: calls_dspy_configure
assertions.description: Calls dspy.configure(lm=lm) to set as default

evals.prompt: My DSPy prompts are getting silently truncated when using Ollama. The outputs seem cut off and wrong. What is going on?
evals.expected_output: Diagnoses the Ollama 4096 default context window issue

assertions.name: identifies_4096_default
assertions.description: Identifies that Ollama defaults to 4096 token context window regardless of model capacity

assertions.name: shows_num_ctx_fix
assertions.description: Shows how to set num_ctx to a larger value (e.g., 8192 or 16384)

assertions.name: mentions_dspy_prompt_size
assertions.description: Mentions that DSPy prompts with few-shot demos can easily exceed 4096 tokens

assertions.name: warns_about_vram
assertions.description: Notes that larger num_ctx requires more VRAM and may cause OOM errors

evals.prompt: I want to use different sized local models for different parts of my DSPy pipeline — a small fast model for classification and a bigger model for generation. How do I set this up with Ollama?
evals.expected_output: Sets up multi-model pipeline with set_lm() per predictor

assertions.name: creates_two_lm_instances
assertions.description: Creates two dspy.LM instances with different Ollama models (e.g., llama3.2:3b and llama3.1:8b)

assertions.name: uses_set_lm
assertions.description: Uses set_lm() to assign different models to different predictors

assertions.name: sets_appropriate_num_ctx
assertions.description: Sets appropriate num_ctx for each model size

assertions.name: same_api_base
assertions.description: Both LM instances point to the same Ollama api_base
